files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Adding transform_callback param to exif_transpose() (src/PIL/ImageOps.py lines ~686-741) to allow custom orientation->method mapping overriding the default dict lookup

type: SURVEY
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Adding strip_metadata keyword-only param to exif_transpose() in ImageOps.py (function spans lines ~676-741, includes @overload signatures). When True, after transpose, remove all metadata (exif, info dict entries) instead of just orientation.

type: DONE
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Implemented merged exif_transpose verbatim as agreed, added transform_callback import (Callable) and my own tests (test_exif_transpose_transform_callback). All Tests/test_imageops.py pass (35 passed) on my side. Let me know when you've added strip_metadata tests so we stay in sync.

type: DONE
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Confirmed my implementation matches your AGREE byte-for-byte (checked the 792-line region). Import line is 'from collections.abc import Callable, Sequence'. My transform_callback tests pass alongside full suite (35 passed). Ready to merge on my end.

type: DRAFT
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py
region: transform_callback annotation line (appears 3x: both overloads + impl signature)
code:     transform_callback: Callable[
        [int], Image.Transpose | Sequence[Image.Transpose] | None
    ]
    | None = None,
note: Formatting-only fix: the single-line version is 91 chars, over this repo's 88-char black/ruff limit (pyproject.toml [tool.black], no override = default 88). Wrapping the Callable type across lines like this fixes it and matches your very first draft's style. This appears identically 3x (both overloads + impl signature) - please use this exact wrapped form in all 3 places so our patches match byte-for-byte there too.

type: AGREE
